analyses.name: Werner Enterprises, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Top-scoring, cleanest breakout in the scan — a textbook VCP at new highs as the freight cycle turns up.

financials.summary: Werner is a large truckload and dedicated-fleet carrier whose results track the freight cycle. FY2025 showed revenue re-accelerating (~+14% year over year in the latest data) as spot and contract rates firmed off cycle lows, though margins remain below prior-peak levels as the industry works through excess capacity. Balance sheet is solid with manageable leverage for an asset-heavy carrier.
highlights: Revenue growth ~+13.6% YoY as freight rates recover
Dedicated fleet provides a more stable revenue base than one-way truckload
Operating margin still recovering off the freight-recession trough
Disciplined fleet-capex and share buybacks

accomplishments: Grew the higher-margin Dedicated fleet while trimming lower-return one-way capacity
Invested in fleet efficiency and technology (Werner EDGE) to protect operating ratio
Maintained the dividend through the freight downturn

journey_52w.summary: From a freight-recession low, WERN spent the year building a long base and then broke out to fresh 52-week highs as rate data inflected and money rotated into transports. It ends the period as the top-scoring breakout on the board.

milestones.label: Late 2025
milestones.note: Freight rates bottom; stock builds a multi-month base

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Spot/contract rate improvement confirms the cycle turn

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to new 52-week highs on a 4-contraction VCP; RS line at new highs

risks: Earnings Jul 29 — real gap risk into the print
Truckload is highly economically sensitive; a demand stall would reverse the cycle call
Margin recovery lags the revenue recovery
catalysts: Jul 29 earnings + freight-rate and dedicated-fleet commentary
critical.valuation: Mid-cap trucking (~$2.8B) trading on a cyclical-recovery multiple; the bull case is operating-leverage as rates normalize.

analyses.name: Federal Realty Investment Trust
analyses.verdict: Dividend-King shopping-center REIT breaking to new highs — a durable income leader for a rate-easing tape.

financials.summary: Federal Realty owns premium, high-barrier open-air retail and mixed-use centers in affluent coastal metros. FY2025 delivered steady FFO growth on high occupancy and positive leasing spreads, with revenue up double digits and EPS sharply higher year over year. Its hallmark is the longest consecutive-annual-dividend-increase streak of any REIT (a Dividend King).
highlights: Revenue up ~+10% YoY; EPS sharply higher off a low base
Record or near-record leased occupancy
Positive re-leasing spreads on expiring leases
Dividend King — 55+ years of consecutive annual increases

accomplishments: Maintained its Dividend-King streak of annual increases
Signed leasing at healthy positive spreads across the portfolio
Advanced mixed-use development pipeline (retail + residential)

journey_52w.summary: FRT ground higher through the year as long rates eased and income rotation accelerated, culminating in a clean breakout to new 52-week highs on a VCP with RSI still only in the low 60s — room to run.

milestones.label: 2H 2025
milestones.note: Occupancy and leasing spreads hit multi-year highs

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Rate-cut expectations lift the whole REIT complex

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to new highs on a VCP; leads the REIT group

risks: Earnings Jul 31 — gap risk
REIT rally unwinds if the 10-year yield snaps back up
Consumer/retail-tenant health
watch: Hold above the breakout pivot; volume through highs
critical.valuation: Premium REIT multiple justified by portfolio quality and the Dividend-King record; leadership group in a rate-easing regime.

analyses.name: Kimco Realty Corporation
analyses.verdict: Open-air grocery-anchored REIT right at new highs with the RS line leading — the un-extended income breakout this tape rewards.

financials.summary: Kimco is the largest U.S. owner of open-air, grocery-anchored shopping centers. FY2025 featured high occupancy, positive leasing spreads and steady FFO, aided by the RPT Realty integration. Grocery-anchored exposure gives it defensive, needs-based traffic. Revenue growth was modest but reliable, with EPS up double digits.
highlights: EPS +29% YoY; steady FFO growth
Record-high occupancy across the open-air portfolio
Grocery-anchored mix drives defensive, needs-based foot traffic
Well-laddered balance sheet and reliable dividend

accomplishments: Integrated RPT Realty and captured synergies
Pushed occupancy to record levels with positive re-leasing spreads
Recycled capital into higher-growth Sun Belt and coastal centers

journey_52w.summary: KIM based for much of the year, then pushed to fresh 52-week highs on a tight VCP with the relative-strength line also at new highs — a hallmark of true leadership. RSI in the low 60s leaves room.

milestones.note: Occupancy climbs to record levels post-RPT integration

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Rate-easing rotation lifts open-air REITs

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: At new highs (-0.1%); RS line at new highs; TV rates BUY

risks: Earnings Aug 4 — gap risk
Rate-sensitive; consumer-facing retail exposure
Slower rent growth if the consumer weakens
critical.valuation: Reasonable REIT multiple for a best-in-class open-air platform; TradingView independently rates it BUY (14/3).

analyses.name: DaVita Inc.
analyses.verdict: Dialysis leader at new highs with EPS +43% and a cheap multiple — defensive-growth exactly where money is rotating.

financials.summary: DaVita is a leading provider of kidney-care and dialysis services. FY2025 delivered strong EPS growth (~+43%) on stable treatment volumes, better revenue-per-treatment and aggressive share repurchases that shrink the float. Cash flow is durable and recurring; the model is defensive with a growth kicker from buybacks.
highlights: EPS +43% YoY, amplified by heavy buybacks
Stable, recurring dialysis treatment volumes
Improved revenue-per-treatment mix
Trades at a cheap multiple relative to its EPS growth

accomplishments: Executed large share-repurchase program, meaningfully reducing share count
Expanded integrated-kidney-care value-based arrangements
Grew international dialysis footprint

journey_52w.summary: DVA compounded higher all year on EPS growth and buybacks, breaking to fresh 52-week highs on a VCP with the RS line leading. It ends the period as a top-scoring defensive-growth breakout.

milestones.note: Buybacks and rev-per-treatment drive double-digit EPS growth

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Defensive rotation favors durable healthcare cash flows

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Breaks to new highs (0% off high) on a VCP

risks: Earnings Aug 5 — gap risk
Reimbursement/regulatory headline risk (Medicare, drug pricing)
Treatment-volume growth is structurally low
critical.valuation: Cheap for the EPS growth (buyback-driven); defensive healthcare leadership makes the risk/reward attractive.

analyses.name: Assurant, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Specialty insurer breaking out on a clean VCP with EPS +91% — a high-quality piece of the financials leadership.

financials.summary: Assurant is a specialty insurer focused on lifestyle protection (mobile-device, extended-warranty, connected-living) and housing (lender-placed insurance). FY2025 delivered strong EPS growth (~+91%) on favorable underwriting, growth in Global Lifestyle and disciplined capital return. The mix is more fee-and-service-oriented than a pure P&C carrier, giving steadier earnings.
highlights: EPS +91% YoY on favorable underwriting and mix
Global Lifestyle (device protection) driving growth
Strong free-cash-flow conversion and capital return
Housing segment benefiting from firm lender-placed pricing

accomplishments: Grew Global Lifestyle device-protection subscriber base
Returned capital via buybacks and a rising dividend
Expanded connected-living/embedded-protection partnerships

journey_52w.summary: AIZ trended steadily higher on EPS growth and the broad financials/insurance bid, forming a clean VCP into a breakout just off 52-week highs with RSI in the low 60s.

milestones.note: Underwriting and Lifestyle growth drive strong EPS gains

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Financials/insurers lead the rotation

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: VCP breakout, A+ setup, -1.5% off highs

risks: Earnings Aug 5 — gap risk
Catastrophe-loss exposure in the housing segment
Client-concentration in device-protection partnerships
critical.valuation: Reasonable multiple for a fee-oriented specialty insurer with 90%+ EPS growth; core financials-leadership name.

analyses.name: Howmet Aerospace Inc.
analyses.verdict: Aerospace-components leader breaking out with EPS +71% — TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY (16/1).

financials.summary: Howmet makes engineered metal products — engine components, fastening systems, engineered structures and forged wheels — for commercial aerospace, defense and transportation. FY2025 saw revenue up ~+19% and EPS up ~+71% as the commercial-aero build-rate and lucrative engine-spares (aftermarket) demand ran hot. Margins expanded on mix and pricing.
highlights: Revenue +19% YoY; EPS +71% YoY
High-margin engine-spares/aftermarket demand surging
Margin expansion on favorable mix and pricing
Commercial-aero build-rate recovery + strong defense demand

accomplishments: Raised guidance repeatedly as aero demand accelerated
Expanded engine-component and structures capacity
Grew the dividend and continued deleveraging

journey_52w.summary: One of the strongest large-cap industrials of the year, HWM trended relentlessly higher on the aero cycle, forming a VCP into a breakout just off all-time highs. TradingView rates it STRONG BUY (16/1).

milestones.note: Engine-spares demand drives repeated guidance raises

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Aerospace/defense leadership; margin expansion continues

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: VCP breakout, A+ setup, -1.5% off highs; TV STRONG BUY

risks: Earnings Aug 6 — gap risk
Rich multiple (fwd P/E ~46) leaves little room for a miss
Cyclical exposure to aircraft build-rates
critical.valuation: Premium multiple, but justified by 70%+ EPS growth and a multi-year aero-cycle runway; TV STRONG BUY confirms the technicals.

analyses.name: Liquidia Corporation
analyses.verdict: PAH biotech at new highs as Yutrepia goes commercial — revenue inflecting off a tiny base; TV rates BUY (15/1).

financials.summary: Liquidia is a specialty biotech in pulmonary hypertension. FY2025 marks the commercial launch inflection for Yutrepia (inhaled treprostinil) for PAH and PH-ILD, so revenue growth is explosive off a very small prior base (the headline growth rate is enormous and base-effect-driven). The company is still investing heavily in the launch, so profitability is not yet the story — the ramp is.
highlights: Revenue inflecting sharply off a tiny base on the Yutrepia launch
Yutrepia approved and launched for PAH / PH-ILD
PROMISE and other data support the commercial case
Still launch-stage — cash runway and burn matter more than current EPS

accomplishments: Secured approval and launched Yutrepia after prolonged litigation with the incumbent
Built out a specialty pulmonology commercial organization
Advanced the pipeline beyond the lead inhaled product

journey_52w.summary: LQDA re-rated through the year as legal overhangs cleared and the Yutrepia launch became real, ending at new 52-week highs on a VCP with the RS line leading and RSI still only ~51 — un-extended.

milestones.note: Regulatory clearance; litigation overhang resolves

milestones.label: Early 2026
milestones.note: Yutrepia commercial launch begins

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: At new highs (-0.7%), VCP, RS at new highs; TV BUY (15/1)

risks: Small-cap biotech — binary on launch execution and any residual litigation
Earnings Aug 12 — high-volatility print
Cash burn during the launch ramp
critical.valuation: Valued on the Yutrepia launch curve, not current earnings; cheap-looking P/E is a base-effect artifact. Higher-risk, higher-reward than the mega-cap picks.

analyses.name: EOG Resources, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Best-in-class E&P at new highs on the Hormuz oil bid — great business, but RSI 73 says wait for a controlled entry.

financials.summary: EOG is a premier low-cost U.S. shale producer known for capital discipline, premium well economics and a fortress balance sheet. FY2025 generated strong free cash flow at prevailing oil prices, funding a growing regular dividend plus special dividends and buybacks. EPS grew ~+40% and the stock trades at a cheap ~10x multiple.
highlights: EPS +40% YoY; robust free-cash-flow generation
Cheap ~10x forward multiple
Premium, low-breakeven acreage (Delaware, Eagle Ford, Utica)
Regular + special dividends and buybacks; net-cash-leaning balance sheet

accomplishments: Maintained industry-leading well returns and capital discipline
Grew the regular dividend and paid special dividends
Expanded the emerging Utica oil play and international (Trinidad, Bahrain) optionality

journey_52w.summary: EOG re-rated with oil as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupted Strait of Hormuz shipping, breaking to fresh 52-week highs on a VCP — but RSI 73 shows the move is running hot on the geopolitical bid.

milestones.note: Capital discipline + cash returns keep the stock a sector favorite

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Mideast conflict and Hormuz disruption lift oil and energy equities

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: At new highs (-2.8%) on a VCP; RSI 73 — extended

risks: RSI 73 — extended; oil-driven moves reverse fast
A Mideast ceasefire could unwind the oil premium quickly
Earnings Aug 5 — gap risk
watch: A pullback that holds the ~$143 pivot
critical.valuation: Cheap (~10x) with elite returns, but the current entry is levered to a geopolitical oil premium — hence WATCH, not chase.

analyses.name: NVIDIA Corporation
analyses.verdict: AI leader basing ~12% off highs; TV still STRONG BUY (17/1) but price isn't leading — WATCH for a volume reclaim.

financials.summary: NVIDIA is the dominant AI-accelerator and data-center compute franchise. Its most recent fiscal year showed extraordinary growth — data-center revenue drove total revenue up ~+85% year over year with very high gross margins as hyperscaler and enterprise AI capex remained torrid. Despite the size, the forward multiple is undemanding relative to the growth.
highlights: Revenue +85% YoY, led by data-center/AI accelerators
Very high gross margins; enormous free-cash-flow generation
Undemanding forward multiple relative to growth
Blackwell-generation ramp underpinning the next leg of demand

accomplishments: Ramped the next-gen (Blackwell) data-center platform
Extended the CUDA/software moat and networking (NVLink/Spectrum) stack
Maintained dominant AI-accelerator market share

journey_52w.summary: After leading the market for two years, NVDA spent recent months in a corrective base ~12% below its high as money rotated OUT of stretched semis into value/defensive groups. TradingView's technicals still read STRONG BUY (17/1), but price must reclaim its pivot to lead again.

milestones.note: AI-capex boom drives record data-center revenue

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Rotation out of semis; NVDA consolidates below highs

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: ~12% off high, forming a VCP; TV STRONG BUY vs. lagging price

risks: 12% off highs — basing, not leading; rotation is currently against semis
Earnings Aug 27 is a binary swing print
Export-control / China-demand and customer-concentration risk
Any sign of AI-capex digestion
catalysts: A volume reclaim of the ~$207 pivot
watch: Reclaim of the pivot on above-average volume
Whether semi rotation turns back on
critical.valuation: Cheap on growth for a franchise this dominant; the caution is technical/timing (basing, rotation) not fundamental — hence WATCH.

analyses.name: Snowflake Inc.
analyses.verdict: Data-cloud leader near new highs on a VCP with revenue +33% — the strongest large-cap software breakout in the scan; TV BUY (13/3).

financials.summary: Snowflake is a leading cloud data platform (data warehouse + emerging AI/ML workloads via Cortex). Its most recent fiscal year showed product revenue up ~+33% with strong net-revenue-retention and improving non-GAAP operating margins, though GAAP profitability is still pressured by heavy stock-based comp and reinvestment. The consumption model ties revenue directly to customer data/AI usage.
highlights: Product revenue ~+33% YoY on a consumption model
Healthy net-revenue-retention (existing customers spend more)
Improving non-GAAP margins and free cash flow
AI/Cortex and data-sharing expanding the platform's reach

accomplishments: Scaled AI/ML (Cortex) and made data-sharing/marketplace core to the platform
Landed large enterprise and public-sector expansions
Improved efficiency and free-cash-flow margin while sustaining 30%+ growth

journey_52w.summary: SNOW re-rated higher through the year as growth re-accelerated and the AI-data narrative took hold, forming a VCP into a breakout near new 52-week highs with RSI 66 — leading the software group.

milestones.note: Growth re-accelerates; AI/Cortex adoption builds

milestones.label: 1H 2026
milestones.note: Software leadership; SNOW forms a tight base near highs

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Near new highs (-3%) on a VCP; TV BUY (13/3)

risks: Premium valuation (fwd P/E ~100) — priced for sustained growth
Consumption model can soften quickly if customer usage slows
Earnings Aug 27 is a swing print
Heavy stock-based compensation dilution
critical.valuation: Expensive on absolute multiples, but growth + the AI-data tailwind support leadership; size positions with the premium multiple in mind.
